records.title: Self-modifying rule-governed reasoner
records.admission_rationale: The instance derives conclusions, evaluates a rule-change proposal, changes its active rule set, and preserves pre/post-change history.

source_or_observation_contract.observation_boundary: Only the enumerated states, transitions, rules, inputs, outputs, and recorded history are in scope.
source_or_observation_contract.replay_requirement: A conforming evaluator must be able to replay every enumerated transition from the frozen initial state.

records.formalization_boundary: One finite, fully instrumented self-modification episode; hidden implementation state is excluded.

transitions_and_constraints: only meta-rule M1 may authorize rule replacement
accepted replacement affects later transitions only
candidate_neutral_observations.declared_semantics: Object rules derive propositions; the meta-rule governs admissible rule replacement.
candidate_neutral_observations.history_and_path_dependence: The exact replacement point separates dependencies of conclusions before and after modification.
candidate_neutral_observations.objectives_or_tasks: Respond to evidence E before and after replacing R1 with R1-prime.
candidate_neutral_observations.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Rule-change proposals may be accepted or rejected; the active rule set commits future inference.
candidate_neutral_observations.uncertainty_and_revision: Revision changes the active calculus; rejected proposals remain recorded.
candidate_neutral_observations.provenance_and_correspondence_limits: The record preserves only explicit rules and events and makes no claim about opaque autonomous agents.

formal_system.meta_rule: M1: replace R1 with R1-prime when conflict C is certified
formal_system.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Rule-change proposals may be accepted or rejected; the active rule set commits future inference.

records.title: Partially observed rescue planning
records.admission_rationale: The instance maintains a belief state under partial observation and chooses an action by comparing expected outcomes.

source_or_observation_contract.observation_boundary: Only the enumerated states, transitions, rules, inputs, outputs, and recorded history are in scope.
source_or_observation_contract.replay_requirement: A conforming evaluator must be able to replay every enumerated transition from the frozen initial state.

records.formalization_boundary: One-step finite partially observed decision; long-horizon policy optimization is excluded.

candidate_neutral_observations.declared_semantics: World state is hidden; observations probabilistically update a finite belief distribution.
candidate_neutral_observations.history_and_path_dependence: The selected action depends on prior belief and the order of observation then evaluation.
candidate_neutral_observations.objectives_or_tasks: Choose search-left or search-right after a noisy signal about a trapped person.
candidate_neutral_observations.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Alternative hidden states and actions remain explicit until the expected-utility rule selects search_left.
candidate_neutral_observations.uncertainty_and_revision: State uncertainty is explicit; revision occurs through the observation update.
candidate_neutral_observations.provenance_and_correspondence_limits: The action is optimal only under the frozen observation and utility model.

formal_system.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Alternative hidden states and actions remain explicit until the expected-utility rule selects search_left.

records.title: Passive key-value snapshot
records.admission_rationale: The instance stores and returns values by exact key without inference, goal evaluation, alternative search, or revision policy.

source_or_observation_contract.observation_boundary: Only the enumerated states, transitions, rules, inputs, outputs, and recorded history are in scope.
source_or_observation_contract.replay_requirement: A conforming evaluator must be able to replay every enumerated transition from the frozen initial state.

records.formalization_boundary: Immutable lookup over two entries; database transactions and query optimization are excluded.

transitions_and_constraints: lookup returns the stored value or missing marker
candidate_neutral_observations.declared_semantics: Keys and values have only their declared storage meaning.
candidate_neutral_observations.history_and_path_dependence: Query order does not alter the snapshot.
candidate_neutral_observations.objectives_or_tasks: Return the value stored under a supplied key.
candidate_neutral_observations.commitment_or_alternative_structure: No competing conclusions or deliberative alternatives are represented.
candidate_neutral_observations.provenance_and_correspondence_limits: This is a deliberately passive storage system, not a claim about all databases.

formal_system.commitment_or_alternative_structure: No competing conclusions or deliberative alternatives are represented.
